content.problemStatement:
Chef has discovered a mysterious square grid representing elevations at each point. It starts raining, and the water level rises over time. At any time ttt, the water level is exactly ttt, which means Chef can only swim through cells with elevations less than or equal to ttt. Chef wants to swim from the top-left corner (0,0)(0, 0)(0,0) to the bottom-right corner (n−1,n−1)(n-1, n-1)(n−1,n−1) of this grid.

Chef can move up, down, left, or right to adjacent cells, but only if both the current cell and the destination cell have elevations at most ttt. Chef swims instantly between reachable cells but must always remain inside the grid. Help Chef find the minimum time ttt when Chef can successfully swim from the start to the destination.

Function DeclarationFunction NamefindMinimumSwimTimefindMinimumSwimTimefindMinimumSwimTime

Parameters
elevationGridelevationGridelevationGrid : A 2D square grid of size n×nn \times nn×n representing elevation levels at each cell.
Return Value
Returns a number representing the minimum time required for Chef to reach the bottom-right cell from the top-left cell.
This value corresponds to the smallest elevation level ttt at which a path exists.
Constraints
1≤n≤501 \leq n \leq 501≤n≤50
0≤elevationGrid[i][j]<n20 \leq \text{elevationGrid}[i][j] < n^20≤elevationGrid[i][j]<n2
All elevation values in elevationGridelevationGridelevationGrid are unique.

Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases.
For each test case:

The first line contains the integer nnn, the size of the grid.
The next nnn lines each contain nnn integers representing the elevation levels of the grid cells.




Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer — the minimum time ttt at which Chef can swim from the top-left to the bottom-right corner of the grid.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. For each test case: The first line contains the integer n, the size of the grid. The next n lines each contain n integers representing the elevation levels of the grid cells.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single integer — the minimum time t at which Chef can swim from the top-left to the bottom-right corner of the grid.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the 4x4 grid, the earliest time to reach bottom-right is when water level reaches 15. The path with minimum max elevation is 0->1->2->5->8->11->14->15, so output is 15.
For the 3x3 grid, the earliest time to reach bottom-right is when water level reaches 8. The path with minimum max elevation is 0->1->2->5->8, so output is 8.
For the 2x2 grid, the earliest time to reach bottom-right is when water level reaches 3. The path with minimum max elevation is 0->1->3, so output is 3.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using a binary search approach along with BFS traversal. We start by initializing the low and high values for the binary search. The low value is set to 0, and the high value is set to the maximum possible elevation value (n^2 - 1). Then, we perform a binary search to find the minimum time t at which Chef can swim from the top-left to the bottom-right corner of the grid. For each mid value in the binary search, we perform a BFS traversal to check if it is possible to reach the bottom-right corner. If it is possible, we update the high value to mid; otherwise, we update the low value to mid + 1.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * n^2 * log(n^2)) per test case, O(T * n^2 * log(n^2)) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n^2) — for the visited matrix and the queue in BFS.
